stories.title: Critics Are Calling Charli XCX's Brat Follow-Up Music, Fashion, Film 'Fascinating'
stories.summary: Charli XCX’s new album Music, Fashion, Film has earned rave reviews since its release on Friday, with critics calling it “fascinating” and “triumphant.” The British pop star shifts from Brat’s dance beats to rock-inspired organic sounds while exploring fame and identity. Some critics remain divided, but the bold follow-up cements her reputation as one of pop’s most daring artists.

stories.title: Tukwila approves camping ban on city property, effective July 28
stories.summary: Tukwila has approved a camping ban on city property, effective July 28, after a 4-3 City Council vote. The ordinance, requested by Police Chief Eric Lund, prohibits camping in public spaces, including parks and sidewalks, with violators facing up to 90 days in jail and a $1,000 fine. The measure aims to address growing encampments in the city's green spaces, with city leaders emphasizing it is not intended to criminalize homelessness but rather provide a legal tool for police response.

stories.title: Tyla drops ‘A*POP,’ her most ambitious project yet
stories.summary: Tyla has released her second album, APOP, marking her most ambitious project yet. The 14-track album blends amapiano, Afrobeats, R&B, and pop, showcasing her evolved artistry. Featuring collaborations with MaWhoo and Babalwa M, it reflects her South African roots and global appeal.

stories.title: Man Utd mascot, six, hit by club's ticket tout crackdown
stories.summary: A six-year-old Manchester United season ticket holder has had restrictions put on his account as part of the club's crackdown on ticket touting, with the club citing repeated access to his account and others as evidence of central control. The boy, who was once a match mascot, will now be unable to apply for away game tickets, despite his mother's appeal being turned down. This is one of thousands of accounts restricted by the club in its operation to identify potential ticket touts.

stories.title: BREAKING: Shiloh Hendrix found GUILTY of disorderly conduct after viral 'racial slur' incident involving Somalis in Minnesota park
stories.summary: Shiloh Hendrix, 37, was found guilty of disorderly conduct Thursday after a Minnesota jury deliberated nine hours in a viral case involving a Somali family. Judge Christa M. Daily sentenced her to a stayed 90-day jail term, 200 hours of community service, and a $1,000 fine for the April 28, 2025 incident at a Rochester playground. Hendrix chased an 8-year-old autistic Somali boy after he took an applesauce packet from her child’s diaper bag and used a racial slur.

stories.title: Why Samsung Gave Its Next Folding Phone a New Shape
stories.summary: Samsung has unveiled a new design for its Galaxy Fold8, a passport-shaped, book-like fold, which is aimed at consumers who prioritize consuming content on their mobile devices. The new phone features a 10:16 aspect ratio for its front cover screen and a 4:3 inner display, making it well-suited for watching videos in both landscape and vertical orientations. This move comes as mobile video watching has seen an explosive growth, with 80 percent of consumers now watching videos on their mobile devices rather than on TV screens or tablets.

stories.title: The 12 Worst Fashion Trends of the Past Century
stories.summary: A 23-year-old singer's revival of the 1950s baby doll dress has sparked controversy, with critics accusing the look of being regressive and childlike. The dress, originally designed as wartime lingerie, has been a recurring fashion trend, with its modern revival drawing attention to the cultural habit of sexualizing young or childlike appearances. The singer has defended the outfit, arguing that the criticism reflects a broader societal issue rather than a personal fashion choice.

stories.title: Regional mayors to take key roles in UK policy decisions as part of No 10 North plans
stories.summary: Regional mayors will take key roles in UK economic policy as part of the new No 10 North initiative, Prime Minister Andy Burnham announced. The revived National Economic Council, chaired by Burnham, will hold its first meeting on Friday, aiming to decentralise decision-making from London. The move seeks to drive growth by aligning central government with regional leaders.

stories.title: ‘It was the moment I lost innocence’: how a childhood stop-and-search led to the British debut movie of the year
stories.summary: A childhood stop-and-search inspired Ishmail Perretta’s debut film “Ish,” a coming-of-age drama set in Luton that critics now compare to classics like “Kes.” The poetic film, praised for its heartfelt portrayal of youth, premieres as Britain’s standout movie of the year. Perretta calls the overwhelmingly positive reviews a relief after fears it might be mistaken for gritty urban dramas.

stories.title: US hits dozens of trading partners with new wave of tariffs
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump's administration has imposed new tariffs of 10% to 12.5% on 60 trading partners, including the UK, China, and the European Union, accounting for nearly all American imports, worth almost 100% of US imports. The tariffs are based on claims that these partners have failed to properly address forced labour, but US trade experts say the move is actually a legal justification for the tariffs. The new duties will take effect immediately, replacing an identical levy that expires on Friday.

stories.title: ‘Jumbo’ Sequel, ‘Queen of Malacca’ Franchise Plans on the Cards as Indonesia’s Visinema Eyes Expansion (EXCLUSIVE)
stories.summary: Indonesian filmmaker Angga Dwimas Sasongko plans to release the fantasy epic "Queen of Malacca" in the first quarter of 2027, after it moves through post-production and attracts interest from potential distribution partners in China, Thailand, and other international markets. The project has gained significant industry attention since its announcement. A sequel to the film, titled "Jumbo," is also reportedly in the works as part of the expanding "Queen of Malacca" franchise.

stories.title: One year, 17 towns, dozens of voices: what we heard along the coast of England and Wales
stories.summary: Youth services in England have seen a 73% decline in funding over the past 12 years, leaving nearly 1 million 16- to 24-year-olds not in work, education or training, with many coastal towns facing severe deprivation and isolation. This has led to young people being unfairly stigmatized as "lazy" or "feral", with their towns being labeled as "problematic". The Against the Tide project will continue to report on the lives of young people in coastal communities across England and Wales over the next year.

stories.title: Burnham to spend first day working from northern branch of No 10
stories.summary: British Prime Minister Rishi Sunak's plan to decentralize power is taking shape as Labour leader Andy Burnham will spend his first day working from the new No 10 North in Manchester, marking a significant shift in power from Whitehall and Westminster to regional areas. Up to 300 civil servants are expected to join ministers Lucy Powell and Louise Haigh at the new site by the end of next year. This move aims to put devolution at the centre of the government's mission to boost local economic growth and address regional disparities.

stories.title: France orders evacuation of peninsula as hundreds flee wildfires by boat
stories.summary: French authorities have ordered the full evacuation of the Cap Ferret peninsula in southwest France as wildfires consume 8,700 hectares near Bordeaux. Hundreds fled by boat as firefighters battle the blaze that began Tuesday, with no new injuries reported Friday. Spain has declared a national emergency amid worsening wildfires near Madrid.

stories.title: What's on for Team NI on first day of Commonwealth Games?
stories.summary: Team Northern Ireland kicks off the 2026 Commonwealth Games in Glasgow with action starting at 08:30 BST as Gary Kelly competes in the men's singles. Swimmers Victoria Catterson, Grace Davison and Lottie Cullen lead the morning heats at Tollcross Swimming Centre, with semi-finals and finals scheduled from 19:00. Boxing’s Louis Rooney also begins his campaign at the SEC Centre.

stories.title: India's 'cockroach' protesters and government to hold talks as stalemate continues
stories.summary: India’s government will hold talks Friday with the ‘Cockroach Janta Party’ as protests demanding education minister Dharmendra Pradhan’s resignation enter their fourth week. Prime Minister Narendra Modi vowed tough new laws on exam paper leaks after activist Sonam Wangchuk ended a 26-day hunger strike, but protesters insist Pradhan must step down. Tens of thousands have marched nationwide since police forcibly removed Wangchuk and injured scores during a Delhi crackdown.

stories.title: ‘A small snippet’: what pubs make of Burnham’s £100m rates giveaway
stories.summary: British Prime Minister Andy Burnham has announced a £100m policy to reduce business rates for pubs, clubs, and live music venues by 20%, as part of a broader agenda to support the high street. This move aims to combat the trend of pub closures, which has seen one pub shut down every day. The policy is expected to benefit thousands of establishments, but its effectiveness in halting the decline of the UK's pub industry remains to be seen.

stories.title: Rare night heron spotted at National Trust site
stories.summary: A rare black-crowned night heron, typically found in Africa, Asia, and the Americas, has been spotted at the National Trust’s Holnicote Estate in Somerset, UK. Conservationists say the sighting follows new freshwater habitats introduced at the site, supporting species like water voles and otters. Experts call it a sign that restored wetlands could help struggling wildlife populations recover.

stories.title: Socceroos star allegedly tests positive for cocaine after speeding
stories.summary: Australia midfielder Cristian Volpato, 22, faces a cocaine allegation after police twice stopped him for speeding in Sydney on Thursday night. He was caught driving over 86km/h in a 60km/h zone at 11:05pm, then again at 1:05am, where he tested positive for cocaine. His international driver’s licence was suspended for six months, and police will continue their investigation.

stories.title: Brent Crude Oil Prices Top $100 as Conflict With Iran Drags On
stories.summary: Stocks in Asia were down following a sell-off in the U.S. markets over soaring oil prices, rising borrowing costs and worries about the technology industry.

stories.title: Play area aims to interest children in rare habitat
stories.summary: A Hampshire science centre has opened a new play area to teach children about England’s rare chalk streams, which face pressure from hot and dry weather. The exhibit at Winchester Science Centre lets kids explore water flow and wildlife in one of the world’s most unique freshwater habitats. The centre hopes early engagement will inspire future conservation efforts.

stories.title: Replacing gas stove can help asthmatics as much as some drugs, US experts find
stories.summary: A US study has found that replacing gas stoves with electric ones can lead to a significant reduction in asthma symptoms, with hospital admissions and days off from work or school dropping by 70% among 72 households in Ohio. This finding is comparable to the benefits reported in clinical trials of commonly used asthma medications. The researchers are now calling for further investigation into the potential health benefits of switching to electric cooking.

stories.title: Female veterans support group makes national impact
stories.summary: Chelley Tulloch, a former RAF medic, credits a female veterans support group with helping her recover from PTSD after multiple war-zone deployments. The group now assists hundreds nationwide as more women join the military, prompting the Ministry of Defence to increase NHS mental health services for veterans.

stories.title: Modi Caught Off Guard as India’s Economic Failings Inflame Young People
stories.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi was busy trying to put out other fires when a student movement suddenly exploded. There are no easy solutions to the problems behind it.

stories.title: Trump Administration Imposed New Tariffs of Around 10% on Over 80 Nations
stories.summary: The administration launched new duties tied to countries’ imports of goods made with forced labor, replacing a 10 percent tariff immediately after it expired.

stories.title: Houthi attacks raise fears of wider Middle East conflict and more global economic damage
stories.summary: Houthi fighters in Yemen have attacked Red Sea shipping, forcing vessels to reroute and threatening the main trade lane between Europe and Asia. US Central Command has conducted 13 consecutive nights of airstrikes on Iranian targets in response. The escalation follows a Saudi nuclear deal raising regional arms race concerns.

stories.title: Trump’s Global Tariffs: What’s the Latest on the Trade War
stories.summary: The president has unveiled a complicated system to forge ahead with his trade war, after the Supreme Court stuck down his original levies.

stories.title: Indonesian babies were trafficked to Singapore. What happens to them now?
stories.summary: An Indonesian court convicted 19 people for trafficking at least 34 babies, with 12 sent to Singapore for adoption. Singaporean parents caring for the babies now face uncertainty over their future after the verdict. Authorities in both countries are expected to decide soon whether the children will remain or return to Indonesia.

stories.title: Google fined €890m by EU for favouring its own apps over rivals
stories.summary: Google has been fined €890m by the EU for allegedly abusing its power by favouring its own apps and services over those from rivals, limiting consumer choice and giving its own services an unfair advantage. The fine is comprised of two separate breaches of the EU's Digital Markets Act, with a total of €460m imposed for a second breach. The company must now comply with the EU's requirements, which may damage services used by millions of European customers.

stories.title: Judge Extends Pause for Paramount-Warner Bros. Deal
stories.summary: The court blocked the companies from closing the acquisition through mid-August, as a judge considers two lawsuits challenging it.

stories.title: Intel Benefits From a New Shift in A.I. Spending
stories.summary: The Silicon Valley chipmaker’s revenue rose 25 percent in the latest quarter, its fastest growth in 15 years, as A.I. firms increasingly bought chips known as central processing units.

Featuring collaborations with MaWhoo and Babalwa M, it reflects her South African roots and global appeal.
